Question: Read the following passage and answer the question: What season is summer in the author's mind?: THE SOUND OF THE KITE_x000D_ _x000D_ I don't know since when, but summer has been imprinted in my mind. Oh. the kite flute... perhaps it will follow me throughout this life...

Question: Read the following text silently: THE STORY OF MOTHER GOOSE One day, the mother goose took her golden goslings for a walk. The goslings began to spread out across the meadow. At that moment, large hailstones fell from the sky. The goslings ran to their mother. The mother goose spread her wings to cover her goslings. Under their wings, the goslings felt warm and comfortable. Then everything became quiet again. The geese immediately insisted: "Let us go, mother!" The mother goose gently lifted her wings and the goslings ran out onto the lawn. They saw their mother's wet wings and tattered feathers. But the light, the meadow, the orange wings, the honeybees made them forget to ask their mother what was wrong. Only one goose, the smallest and weakest, ran to her mother and asked: "Why are your wings so torn?" The mother goose softly replied: "Everything is fine, my child!" The goslings scattered on the grass again and the mother goose felt happy. Question: Silently read the following text: DA VILLAGE SCENE Winter has truly arrived! Clouds from above slide down the mountain slopes, occasionally scattering a light rain on the silver-white thatched roofs. The yellow mustard flowers appear in long strips in the mist on the hillside. The noisy, winding stream has shrunk, revealing smooth and clean pebbles. On the water surface, only the long-legged spiders like busy and happy pincers compete to go upstream. On the tops of the old, ancient trees, the last remaining yellow leaves are rustling before saying goodbye to their simple mother. But the rows of Da village areca trees, despite all the brutal power of winter, still have their leaves hanging gently like swallow tails. On the ground hardened by the cold, the young leaves are still spreading, pale yellow and the areca trees are still graceful, swaying their bodies, as if they were born to decorate Da village with a more elegant and gentle look.
